nav.entitiesHint: View and manage all world entities — NPCs, factions, locations, items.
nav.graphHint: Visual relationship graph of your world. Drag, zoom, explore connections.
nav.assistantHint: AI co-author grounded in your lore. Chat, generate, review drafts.
nav.settingsHint: Project settings, knowledge base, integrations, and token usage.

projects.title: Projects
projects.emptyBody: Create your first project — or import an existing one from an export file.
projects.deleteConfirmBody: All entities, relationships and attachments of "{{name}}" will be deleted forever. This cannot be undone.

entities.title: Entities
entities.noEntities: No entities yet. Create one — or ask the assistant to propose starting lore.
entities.noMatches: Nothing found — change the query or reset the filter.

entityEdit.deleteConfirmBody: "{{title}}" and all of its fields and relationships will be deleted forever.

entityDetail.deleteConfirmBody: "{{title}}" and all of its fields and relationships will be deleted forever.

graph.pickRoot: Pick a root entity to view its neighborhood.
graph.emptyWorld: Your world is empty — describe it in the AI panel and review the generated starting lore.
graph.largeWorldNotice: Large world — Focused mode may be easier to navigate.
graph.resetLayoutConfirmBody: Every manually placed node in view will be rearranged. This can't be undone.

fields.showOnCardTitle: Show this field on the graph card

icon.saveFirst: Save the entity first to set an icon.

richText.saveFirstForImage: Save the entity first to insert images

projectSettings.heading: Project settings
projectSettings.instructionsPlaceholder: E.g. "Write NPC descriptions in the second person", "Always add a 'plot hook' field", "Keep a dark, gothic tone".
projectSettings.instructionsHint: Free-form notes on style, tone and format — blended into the assistant's and lore-generation system prompts for this project.
projectSettings.reindexHint: The search index is rebuilt from your entities automatically, but if the assistant can't find something that clearly exists, rebuilding it here fixes that.

usage.heading: Token usage
usage.hint: Cumulative token spend for this project across all AI assistant turns, lore generation and verification runs.

knowledge.heading: Project knowledge base
knowledge.hint: Upload reference documents (rulebooks, player's handbook, a setting overview — PDF, .txt, .md, .json, .csv, .yaml and other text formats). The assistant uses them when generating lore and can search them in chat, but they never become world canon.

onboarding.restartBody: The running backend doesn't expose the AI Assistant API yet (the config endpoint returned 404). Restart it to pick up the new code:
onboarding.setupBody1: The assistant runs on your own LLM API key (BYOK) — your lore never leaves this machine except for the calls you make to your chosen provider. Current provider: {{provider}}.
onboarding.setupBody3: Then restart the backend. The key is masked in all logs and errors.

assistant.emptyInviteEmptyWorld: Your world is empty. Describe it in a couple of sentences — the assistant will propose starting lore: locations, factions, characters and their connections. Nothing becomes canon without your approval. You can also just ask questions.
assistant.emptyInviteHasWorld: Ask about the world, ask it to expand a part of it, or add new lore — the assistant answers grounded in existing canon and proposes drafts for review.
assistant.inputPlaceholderBlocked: Finish reviewing the draft above first (approve / request changes / reject)
assistant.inputPlaceholderDefault: Ask about the world or request new lore… (Enter to send)
assistant.attachTitle: Attach a file to this message (image, PDF, text)
assistant.hintIsolatedInstruction: Weave «{{title}}» into the world: figure out who and what connects to it, and add the missing entities and relationships.

review.newBadgeTitle: Entirely new — not based on existing lore
review.feedbackPlaceholder: What should change? E.g. "make the guild sinister, give it a secret leader"
review.previewBanner: This is a preview — it enters the world only after you approve.
review.groundedIn_one: Grounded in {{count}} fragment of existing lore
review.groundedIn_other: Grounded in {{count}} fragments of existing lore

warnings.budget_exhausted: Token budget for this run is exhausted — generation stopped early.
warnings.mentions_existing: The request mentions existing entity «{{title}}» ({{type}}) — the draft should connect to it, not duplicate it.
warnings.duplicate_in_batch: The draft contains two near-identical titles: «{{title_a}}» and «{{title_b}}».
warnings.dropped_unknown_source: Dropped a relationship from an unknown source ({{ref}}).
warnings.dropped_unknown_target: Dropped a relationship to an unknown target ({{ref}}) — not a draft entity or retrieved lore.
warnings.uncited_lore_id: The draft cites lore id {{id}} that was never retrieved — treat the related claim as unverified.

events.budget_exhausted_reply: Token budget for this conversation is exhausted — start a new session to continue.
events.batch_committed: Done — added {{count}} entities to the world ({{titles}}) and {{edges}} relationships.
events.batch_rejected: Draft rejected — nothing was written to the world.

integrations.heading: Integrations
integrations.hint: Connect external tools (Obsidian vault, Foundry VTT, LongStoryShort) to push and pull content between Loregraph and your other apps.
integrations.noConnections: No connections yet. Add one to get started.
integrations.deleteBody: The connection "{{name}}" will be removed. External data is not affected.
integrations.exportNothing: Nothing to export — all entities are already up to date or none are selected.
integrations.importHint: Import will read from the configured source and create or update entities.
integrations.importInvalidJson: Invalid JSON — check the syntax and try again.

errors.invalid_edge_reference: That relationship points at an entity that no longer exists.
errors.cross_project_edge: Can't link entities that belong to different projects.
errors.unsupported_export_format: This export file's format isn't supported by this version.
errors.invalid_icon_reference: That attachment doesn't belong to this entity.
errors.chat_attachment_limit_exceeded: Too many or too large attachments for one message.
errors.configuration: The AI assistant isn't configured yet — set an API key in backend/.env and restart.
errors.generation: The AI model didn't return a usable result — try again.
errors.awaiting_review_conflict: A draft is awaiting review — approve, reject or request changes before sending new messages.
errors.not_awaiting_review: This conversation isn't waiting on a review decision.
errors.timeout: The assistant took too long to respond — try again.
errors.connector_unavailable: The external tool is unreachable — check the connection settings.
errors.unsupported_connector_capability: This connector doesn't support this operation.
errors.external_data_parse: Failed to parse data from the external source.

tooltips.depth: How many relationship hops to show from the root entity.
tooltips.viewModeFocused: Show only the root entity and its direct connections.
tooltips.viewModeAll: Show every entity in the project at once.
tooltips.showOnCard: Show this field on the entity's graph card.
tooltips.fieldType: Choose how this field's value is stored and displayed.
tooltips.contextSelector: Focus the assistant on a specific entity, or ask about the whole world.
tooltips.attachFile: Attach an image, PDF, or text file to this message.
tooltips.reviewApprove: Write the approved entities and relationships to the world canon.
tooltips.reviewRequestChanges: Send feedback to the assistant — it will revise the draft.
tooltips.reviewReject: Discard this draft. Nothing will be written to the world.
tooltips.agentInstructions: Free-form notes on style, tone and format — blended into every AI prompt for this project.
tooltips.reindex: Rebuild the search index from your entities. Useful if the assistant can't find something that exists.
tooltips.knowledgeUpload: Upload reference documents (rulebooks, setting overviews). They help the assistant ground its responses but never become world canon.
tooltips.integrationGrounding: When enabled, the assistant retrieves lore from this connection's source for grounding.
tooltips.integrationAutoPush: Automatically push approved drafts to this connection after commit.

help.title: Help
help.gettingStartedBody: Loregraph is a local, self-hosted app for building and running tabletop RPG campaigns. You create **entities** (NPCs, factions, locations, items) and connect them with **relationships** in a visual graph. The AI assistant helps you expand your world, grounded in what already exists.
help.creatingProjectBody: From the Projects page, click **New project**. Give it a name and optional description. Your project stores all entities, relationships, and settings in a local SQLite database — nothing leaves your machine.
help.entitiesBody: Entities are the building blocks of your world: characters, places, factions, items, events — anything with a name and type.
help.entityTypesBody: Types are freeform strings (e.g. `NPC`, `Faction`, `Location`). They determine the color on the graph. Create new types simply by typing a new name when creating an entity.
help.entityFieldsBody: Each entity has custom key-value fields: **Text**, **Rich text** (with images and formatting), **Number**, or **Tags**. Toggle **"on card"** to show a field on the graph node.
help.entityIconsBody: Set a square icon for any entity — it appears on the graph card and in lists. Crop to square after upload.
help.graphViewBody: The graph visualizes all entities and their relationships. Two modes:
help.focusedMode: **Focused** — pick a root entity and explore its neighborhood. Adjust **depth** (1–5 hops) and filter by **edge type**.
help.allMode: **All** — see every entity in the project at once. Best for small worlds; use the nudge to switch to Focused when it gets large.
help.graphInteractions: Click a node to open its detail panel. Drag to reposition. Scroll to zoom. Right-click or drag between nodes to create a relationship.
help.aiAssistantBody: The assistant is an AI co-author that knows your world through retrieval — it never invents facts outside your canon.
help.assistantSetupBody: Create `backend/.env` with your API key (Anthropic, OpenAI, or Ollama for local). Without a key, the manual editor works fully but the assistant tab shows setup instructions.
help.assistantChatBody: Ask questions about your world, request new lore, or attach reference files. Use the **Context** selector to focus on a specific entity or the whole world.
help.assistantDraftsBody: When the assistant proposes new entities, you see a **draft review card** with:
help.draftReviewList:
- Per-entity titles and summaries (editable)
- Relationship web between new and existing entities
- Warnings about duplicates or ungrounded claims
- Token cost estimate
help.draftActions: **Approve** writes everything to canon. **Request changes** sends your feedback for revision. **Reject** discards the draft.
help.knowledgeBaseBody: Upload reference documents (PDFs, text files, Markdown) on the Settings page. The assistant uses them for grounding during generation and chat, but they never become world canon themselves.
help.integrationsBody: Connect external tools to push/pull content:
help.integrationObsidian: - **Obsidian** — writes `[[wikilink]]` Markdown files to a vault folder
help.integrationFoundry: - **Foundry VTT** — connects via MCP bridge for actors, journals, and scenes
help.settingsBody: The Settings page has three sections:
help.settingsAgent: - **Agent instructions** — style/tone notes blended into every AI prompt
help.settingsUsage: - **Token usage** — cumulative AI token spend for this project
